title: Physiotherapist*(9209) - Alexandra Hospital
description:
Overview

The Physiotherapist is responsible for the assessment and management of patients referred for physiotherapy.  Under supervision, assist in areas such as clinical supervision, research, professional education and induction.

Job Responsibilities

  • Assess and manage patients referred for physiotherapy independently and implement appropriate treatment plans
  • Document patient physiotherapy records accurately
  • Charge patients accurately and update individual performance statistical records regularly
  • Comply with clinical and service standards
  • Supervise Therapist Assistants in the workgroup
  • Assist with induction of new physiotherapists deployed to the workgroup
  • Supervise students on clinical attachment
  • Practice within areas of professional competence and seek clinical support from supervisor / other healthcare professionals when appropriate
  • Collaborate with other healthcare members and participate in multidisciplinary discussions.
  • Assist and/or co-lead in projects assigned to the workgroup
  • Manage and oversee therapy equipment inventory
  • Review and update clinical protocols and SOPs
  • Assist in developing, promoting and marketing new clinical services
  • Contribute to departmental in-service training programs, hospital educational talks and external talks / workshops / forums / seminars
  • Actively update physiotherapy knowledge and skills

Requirements

  • Recognised Degree in Physiotherapy
  • Good communication and interpersonal skills
  • Service oriented and customer focused
  • Team player
  • 1-3 years of relevant work experience and full AHPC registration preferred

If the role sounds interesting to you, please contact ‘careers_alexandra@nuhs.edu.sg’ to understand more on the role. Please indicate the position that you are interested in the subject heading.

*Only Singapore Citizens and Singapore Permanent Residents may apply.

postedCompany.description:
The National University Health System (NUHS) plays a key, contributing role in Singapore’s healthcare. Our versatile and experienced team of healthcare professionals has only one aim - to help people get better, and live healthier and longer lives.

We do this by improving medical care, lowering disease recurrence, producing better healthcare professionals, and turning research findings into better medical treatments for diseases that affect Singaporeans. And the reason we’re able to do these things is because we have the best and most dedicated people. Come join us and help shape medicine for our future.
